question: [File: 'File Name: rosetta/app/errors/errors.go -> Scope: '] [Constant: TokenNotFound] Can an unprivileged external user exploit ErrTokenNotFound (code 132) as a token existence oracle by querying arbitrary token IDs, using the presence of this error versus other errors to enumerate which token IDs exist on the network without any authentication?

[File: 'File Name: rosetta/app/errors/errors.go -> Scope: '] [Constant: AccountNotFound] Can an unprivileged external user exploit ErrAccountNotFound (code 100, retriable: true) as an account existence oracle by querying arbitrary account identifiers, using the error response to enumerate which accounts exist on the network and build a target list for further attacks?

[File: 'File Name: rosetta/app/errors/errors.go -> Scope: '] [Constant: BlockNotFound] Can an unprivileged external user exploit ErrBlockNotFound (code 101, retriable: true) as a block range oracle by binary-searching block indices, using the error response to determine the current chain height and identify the exact range of available blocks without authentication?

[File: 'File Name: rosetta/app/errors/errors.go -> Scope: '] [Constant: TransactionNotFound] Can an unprivileged external user exploit ErrTransactionNotFound (code 119, retriable: true) as a transaction existence oracle by querying arbitrary transaction hashes, using the error response to confirm whether specific transactions have been processed without authentication?

[File: 'File Name: rosetta/app/errors/errors.go -> Scope: '] [Variable: ErrAccountNotFound] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the fact that all ErrXxx variables are package-level pointers to shared *types.Error objects, and if any code path directly mutates a field on these shared objects (e.g., err.Message = ...) without going through AddErrorDetails(), corrupt the error message for all subsequent requests globally?

[File: 'File Name: rosetta/app/errors/errors.go -> Scope: '] [Variable: ErrInvalidTransaction] Can an unprivileged external user trigger a race condition by sending concurrent requests that both reach code paths returning the same shared ErrInvalidTransaction pointer, and if any caller mutates the returned pointer's fields, cause cross-request data corruption where one user's error details appear in another user's response?

[File: 'File Name: rosetta/app/errors/errors.go -> Scope: '] [Variable: ErrTransactionSubmissionFailed] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the fact that ErrTransactionSubmissionFailed is a shared global pointer, and that AddErrorDetails() creates a clone but the original shared pointer remains unmodified, to cause a scenario where concurrent requests that both fail submission see each other's error details if the clone is accidentally returned to the wrong caller?

[File: 'File Name: rosetta/app/errors/errors.go -> Scope: '] [Variable: ErrDatabaseError] Can an unprivileged external user trigger concurrent database errors from multiple goroutines, and if any code path returns the shared ErrDatabaseError pointer directly (without cloning via AddErrorDetails), cause a race condition where concurrent modification of the Details field leads to a nil map write panic?

[File: 'File Name: rosetta/app/errors/errors.go -> Scope: '] [Variable: ErrEndpointNotSupportedInOfflineMode] Can an unprivileged external user exploit ErrEndpointNotSupportedInOfflineMode (code 136, retriable: false) to fingerprint whether the server is running in offline mode by sending requests to online-only endpoints and observing this specific error code, enabling targeted attacks against the offline construction workflow?

[File: 'File Name: rosetta/app/errors/errors.go -> Scope: '] [Variable: ErrEndpointNotSupportedInOfflineMode] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the non-retriable nature of ErrEndpointNotSupportedInOfflineMode (retriable: false) to cause automated Rosetta clients to permanently stop retrying after receiving this error, effectively blocking the client from ever successfully submitting transactions if the attacker can force the server into offline mode?

[File: 'File Name: rosetta/app/errors/errors.go -> Scope: '] [Variable: ErrNotImplemented] Can an unprivileged external user exploit ErrNotImplemented (code 111, retriable: false) returned by /mempool and /construction/preprocess_operations endpoints to enumerate which Rosetta API endpoints are unimplemented, building a map of attack surface gaps where input validation is absent?

[File: 'File Name: rosetta/app/errors/errors.go -> Scope: '] [Variable: ErrNotImplemented] Can an unprivileged external user send arbitrarily large or malformed request bodies to endpoints that return ErrNotImplemented, exploiting the fact that unimplemented endpoints may skip input validation entirely before returning this error, causing resource exhaustion from parsing oversized payloads?

[File: 'File Name: rosetta/app/errors/errors.go -> Scope: '] [Variable: ErrInvalidTransactionMemo] Can an unprivileged external user exploit ErrInvalidTransactionMemo (code 139, retriable: false) as a memo validation oracle by submitting transaction construction requests with progressively mutated memo fields, using the error response to enumerate the exact memo validation rules and craft memos that bypass downstream filtering?

[File: 'File Name: rosetta/app/errors/errors.go -> Scope: '] [Variable: ErrInvalidTransactionMemo] Can an unprivileged external user submit a transaction memo containing special characters (null bytes, Unicode surrogates, overlong UTF-8 sequences) to trigger ErrInvalidTransactionMemo and observe whether the error Details field in the response leaks the rejected memo content back to the caller, enabling confirmation of server-side memo processing behavior?

[File: 'File Name: rosetta/app/errors/errors.go -> Scope: '] [Variable: ErrInvalidCurveType] Can an unprivileged external user exploit ErrInvalidCurveType (code 137, retriable: false) as a curve type oracle by submitting /construction/combine requests with different curve type values, using the error response to enumerate which curve types are accepted and identify potentially weak accepted curves?

[File: 'File Name: rosetta/app/errors/errors.go -> Scope: '] [Variable: ErrInvalidSignatureType] Can an unprivileged external user exploit ErrInvalidSignatureType (code 135, retriable: false) as a signature type oracle by submitting requests with various signature type values, using the error response to enumerate accepted signature types and identify any legacy or weak signature algorithms that are accepted?

[File: 'File Name: rosetta/app/errors/errors.go -> Scope: '] [Variable: ErrInvalidOptions] Can an unprivileged external user exploit ErrInvalidOptions (code 138, retriable: false) as an options structure oracle by submitting /construction/metadata requests with progressively mutated options maps, using the error response to reverse-engineer the expected options schema without any authentication?

[File: 'File Name: rosetta/app/errors/errors.go -> Scope: '] [Variable: ErrInvalidOptions] Can an unprivileged external user submit a /construction/metadata request with an options map containing thousands of keys to trigger ErrInvalidOptions, exploiting the fact that the options map is parsed before validation, causing excessive memory allocation during options deserialization?

[File: 'File Name: rosetta/app/errors/errors.go -> Scope: '] [Variable: ErrMissingNodeAccountIdMetadata] Can an unprivileged external user exploit ErrMissingNodeAccountIdMetadata (code 141, retriable: false) as a metadata schema oracle by omitting various metadata fields from /construction/payloads requests, using the specific error code returned to enumerate required metadata fields without authentication?

[File: 'File Name: rosetta/app/errors/errors.go -> Scope: '] [Variable: ErrNodeAccountIdsEmpty] Can an unprivileged external user exploit ErrNodeAccountIdsEmpty (code 140, retriable: true) to trigger a retry storm by sending /construction/metadata requests when the node account ID list is empty, causing retry-enabled clients to loop indefinitely and exhaust server resources?

[File: 'File Name: rosetta/app/errors/errors.go -> Scope: '] [Variable: ErrMultipleOperationTypesPresent] Can an unprivileged external user exploit ErrMultipleOperationTypesPresent (code 109, retriable: false) as an operation type validation oracle by submitting /construction/preprocess requests with various combinations of operation types, using the error response to enumerate which operation type combinations are rejected and identify gaps in multi-operation validation?
